channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:summary: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations.

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Dinner night at Miocene supports Chelsea Lewis Memorial Award for women in the trades and [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] New partnership allows B.C. patients to access surgeries in Alberta, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Indigenous Lands & Innovation Topics include: satellite, precision agriculture, renewable energy, AI, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Dinner night at Miocene supports Che...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] New partnership allows B.C. patie...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Fire at Picnic Gazebo in Williams Lake quic...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Calls grow for public inquiry after Tumbler...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] 13 B.C. drug-user groups lose bid fo...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Quesnel RCMP-Search and Rescue respond to r...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Cariboo sailor brings Arctic and ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Similkameen First Nations denounce M...

Additional sources provided:

  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] As climate, industry warm Okanagan waterways, e...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Picnic gazebo at Williams Lake Boita...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Sumo AI
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechRadar] 'Big tech pays for power — why not broadband?': Hyperscalers signed the rate protection pledge for electricity infrastructure, so should they do it for broadband? An expert chimes in and [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. allowing Vancouver and Victoria to create independent police academies, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Community Tech & Governance Topics include: broadband, data sovereignty, AI, infrastructure, community ownership.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Big tech pays for power — why not broadband?': ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. allowing Vancouver and Victoria...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] YouTube Adds Tool to Help Public Figures Repo...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Dutch MPs call for free government-backed VPN, a...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] CNC Fort St James campus to close its doors...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] New detection system in Chilliwac...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] New mental health crisis helpline...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] One Key Protein Could Be a Powerful New Targe...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] We Finally Know How Bumblebee Queens Can Surv...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Williams Lake city councillor to propose “a...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Rally at Surrey border park to mark ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Princeton man who attacked doctor ha...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] U.S. Ambassador Hoekstra says he and...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Former Chilcotin chief guilty on 2 o...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Heavy police presence Tuesday evenin...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Film Club presents Osc...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'People don’t trust bad AI voices': listeners ra...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'AI has reinvented computing and is driving the ...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Who You Gonna Call? Not 911 on BC’s Central Coast
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Pride groups call for censure and resignati...
  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] The Modern Times cafe moved to a pay-what-you-w...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Our First Look at the New St. Paul’s Hospital
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Acting merit commissioner selected f...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] MLA Lorne Doerkson responds to Human...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Sumo AI
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [My Cariboo Now] B.C. tables legislation to expand protest restrictions near schools and places of worship and [ScienceAlert] Pesticide Exposure Could Increase Disease Risk For The Next 20 Generations, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Working Lands & Industry Topics include: fiber, satellite, precision agriculture, solar, AI.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] B.C. tables legislation to expand protest r...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Pesticide Exposure Could Increase Disease Ris...

  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Bio-based, recyclable resins outperform their fo...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] How I turned my Starlink Mini into the ultimate off-...

  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Trees Seen Emitting a Ghostly Light During a ...

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Victoria charity launches national youth contest to fight book bans and [Neowin] Planet of Lana II review: Gorgeous sci-fi adventure that ends too soon, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling Topics include: AI, infrastructure, maintenance, funding.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Victoria charity launches national y...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Planet of Lana II review: Gorgeous sci-fi adventure...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Eby calls for more AI regulation aft...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] FORSTER: Log Cabin Quilters donate to ...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Someone converted an Ikea wall lamp into a ...

  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] If Alien Dyson Spheres Are Real, These Are the Sta...

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] International Women’s Day 2026: Marg...

  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Man linked to Edgewood ostrich pr...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Sleep experts say permanent DST could bring...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Three year investigation leads to two arres...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] BC Teachers vote to ratify new collective a...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] B.C. teen drowned after dingo att...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Accused Naramata murderer’s trial...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Before The Matrix and The Terminator, there was ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] International Women’s Day 2026: Bobb...

item.title: Resilient Rural Futures
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Resilient Rural Futures.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [XDA Developers] Please stop relying on the Internet for your smart home devices and [XDA Developers] I blocked all cloud access from my smart home for a week to see what still works, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Resilient Rural Futures Topics include: broadband, fiber, telemedicine, solar, AI.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Please stop relying on the Internet for you...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I blocked all cloud access from my smart ho...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Feds commit $70.4M for worker traini...
  • U.S. News & World Report: [U.S. News & World Report] Telemedicine Not Closing the Ment...

Additional sources provided:

  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I self-hosted everything for a year, and th...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] EDITOR: Alternative available for Yell...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] What Can You Do with Used Plastic and 3D Printers...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] TerraPower Nuclear Reactor in Wyoming Gets...
  • The Globe and Mail: [The Globe and Mail] Tumbler Ridge shooting highlights rural...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] 6 things I've learned after a decade of using ...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Sumo AI
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Cariboo Voices And Local News
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Urgent and Primary Care Centre celebrates one year of service and [My Cariboo Now] Early morning vehicle incident claims life of a pedestrian in Quesnel, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Cariboo Voices & Local News Topics include: broadband, AI, digital divide, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Urgent and Primary Car...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Early morning vehicle incident claims life ...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] 100 Mile Performing Arts Society’s aud...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Quesnel River Rush wins one, lose...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Quesnel Figure Skating Club sends...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] RCMP find ‘no credible threat’ to...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] 100 Mile House RCMP find ‘no credible ...

Additional sources provided:

  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Pedestrian dies in early-morning ...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Quesnel is getting ready to party like it’s...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Williams Lake RCMP seeking help to find mis...

Credits

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [My Cariboo Now] Quesnel and PG Search and Rescue rescue trapped snowmobiler and [Resilience.org] The Future of Forests, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life Topics include: AI, infrastructure, maintenance, funding.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Quesnel and PG Search and Rescue rescue tra...
  • Resilience.org: [Resilience.org] The Future of Forests
  • The Verge: [The Verge] The Rubin Observatory’s alert system sent 800,00...
  • Hackster.io: [Hackster.io] The youyeetoo LoraSecrt Is a Meshtastic-Inspir...
  • CNET: [CNET] AI Data Centers: What to Know About Their Water and E...
  • Seeded Content: TRAILBLAZER CHRONICLES: What happens when business shows up ...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Royal Canadian Air Force helps Norther...

Additional sources provided:

  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Williams Lake saw a wetter February than Qu...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] BC’s Forests Minister on ‘Tough Days,’ Wildfire, ...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] The War on Poaching Has Gone Full Tech Dystopia—an...
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Budget 2026 supports wildfire-resilient forest...

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Historic sex crimes trial wraps up in Williams Lake and [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake screening of The Spirit Who Swims sparks salmon conservation dialogue, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Indigenous Lands & Innovation Topics include: fiber, connectivity, AI, infrastructure, maintenance.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [My Cariboo Now] Nicole Audet officially declared as new CRD Area A Director and [My Cariboo Now] B.C. to end twice-yearly time changes with move to permanent Daylight Saving Time, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Community Tech & Governance Topics include: renewable energy, solar, AI, infrastructure, maintenance.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Nicole Audet officially declared as new CRD...

  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Nominations open for 2026 Best of the ...

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [My Cariboo Now] Wildfire Risk Reduction project scheduled near Ten Mile Lake and [BC Gov News] Supporting tech companies to boost B.C. critical minerals sector, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Working Lands & Industry Topics include: satellite, precision agriculture, solar, data sovereignty, AI.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).
